title: Security Scan on Agent Stop
description: After the agent finishes a task, scan changed files for hardcoded secrets, credentials, and obvious security issues.

action.prompt:
A coding task just completed. Perform a quick security check on any files changed in this session:

1. Run: `git diff --name-only HEAD` to identify changed files
2. For each changed file, check for:
   - Hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, or secrets (regex: `(?i)(api_key|secret|password|token|private_key)\s*=\s*['"][^'"]{8,}`)
   - Credentials committed to source (any string that looks like a secret value)
   - CORS set to `*` on new endpoints
   - New `eval()`, `exec()`, or `shell=True` usage
   - SQL queries built with string concatenation
3. If any issues are found, report them with file:line and severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM)
4. If no issues found, output: `Security check: CLEAN`

This is a lightweight fast check — not a full audit. Flag anything suspicious for human review.
